item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image is owned by Alamy, and requires purchase for official use. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like two stories in one. I love how in depth you went. Plus I like the thought of the rancher constantly beating up the molester. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe earlier drafts were considerably more esoteric, but I understood this perfectly and loved it. Little sad that -A-1 hadn't deliberately exploited it, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He doesn't understand or really care about Earth animals; they all just kind of run together for him. He keeps referring to it as "heat" because that's a consistent term for what he actually cares about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes sense, but in a transcript where the being uses 5 different terms to refer to 1 very important mammal, the resource is only ever called "heat". It comes across as intentionally specific, is what I'm saying, which has the effect of discouraging the reader from considering the possibility that it means something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's basically a slangish term used by the organism for something they need from humans. It's not literally heat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as I love this, and I really do…there's a SUN a mere 8 light minutes away and they can only get the heat they need from humans? In Florida?

I could see if "heat" was just an approximation of a term from their language that means "human life force" or some mumbo jumbo, but there's no indication of that. It reads like "heat" is just what it sounds like, and I'm just not following.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1807-1 is the space within the okapi, measuring approximately 2km2 in area.

Aaaaand upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, yes. This is nice. I was first a bit baffled with another extradimensional "small to large" space, similar to 108, but the diving suit folks sealed the deal. It's not superb, but certainly a bit above average SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes much more sense now. And its even cooler. You need to find a way to make it clearer that that's how it happened. I like minimalism as much as the next guy, but these details really add to the SCP, and it's hard to deduce the series of events with the little information given.

Edit: Upon reading this article for the third time (because I love this SCP) I realize that I misread key parts. I still think making it a little more obvious is a good idea, but I no longer feel so adamantly about it. If you feel that it improves your piece to make the reader work for their meal, then that is your artistic decision. Regardless, kudos. This is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that "child-eating horse" should be "child-eating deer", since okapi are only distantly related to horses and hardly resemble them at all. I know they're closer to giraffes genetically, but it looks like a deer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…okami in a petting zoo…"

Whether that's the great god Amaretsu or just a regular Japanese wolf, it'll be a lot more intense than most petting zoos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads much better now! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, that makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why that specific? Is that the official non-euclidian okapi corpse portal holding cell?

Yes. The non-Euclidean lemur corpse holding cell is #8475. God help you if you get the two confused!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Costumes" implies they aren't actually functional for diving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rather like this. The interdimensional okapi that absorbs heat from humans and traps them inside its own pocket universe. The picture is rather nice too.

On another note, "vintage diving costumes" sound somewhat anticlimactic to me. "Attire resembling vintage diving costumes" would sound better in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Well done.

Starting the article, I didn't think much of it, but the interview log made it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've addressed some of the problems brought up in this thread; as for the people asking for more information on the -B's and such, I'm afraid the article is getting cluttered enough as it is; however, I do think that it's quite possible that I might expound on these things in a tale in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"This internal space is hereby designated SCP-1807-1."

"the anomalous interior of the okapi, hereby designated SCP-1807-1"

"SCP-1807-1 is the space within the okapi"

Seems 1807-1 is over-designated. I found this distracting. Otherwise, a pretty enjoyable little oddment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this needs more explanation of -B and his reasoning for taking the people (I would also be happy with a tale for it, though), but overall it's pretty good, and very weird.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. And the okapi was a nice touch right in the end. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this enough to upvote (also I'm drunk), but it needs some work. The paragraph describing -1 should be moved right up under where it's first defined, after the first paragraph of the description. Also, I can't imagine the Foundation saying something like "People who touch it become voices", because that's not usually the sort of thing we go in for. All that can be proven is the people disappear and then something claiming to be them or sounding like them or whatnot appears in the okapi.

(But holy crap seriously, this whole thing is totally amazing. You've just got some tone issues.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get it now. I really like the idea, actually, but I still think it needs more clarity as to whats happening. It's just _really_ confusing. There are some other things that need to be cleaned up. I'm new so take all of this with a grain of salt:

Long line-by-line
Close

I think you should ditch the pedophile completely, it distracts from the crux of the matter.
I think, before the logs, you should add in something about a petting zoo, like an ear tag or even an origin story with the disappearing children. It doesn't give anything away that the readers don't already know, and it will make the big reveal at the end all the more likely to succeed. This thing is weird enough that no one is going to guess it by just reading about a petting zoo.

As for the clunky writing:

 Unless instructed otherwise for the purposes of testing, personnel are not to make unprotected skin contact with the object.

Is it an object? I think the best word would be 'specimen.' Although most animal autopsy reports use the word 'carcass,' it sounds too clunky, and you don't want to give away that it's a "corpse" just yet.

 The object measures approximately 1m in height and 60kg in weight.

'Specimen' or 'body' should be used here, not "object."

 This is only true for unprotected contact of the subject's skin on the exterior of the okapi's form; contact with the object through clothing and contact with the interior of SCP-1807 do not activate this property.

This sentence is clunky. I suggest you get rid of the first part entirely, starting with "Contact with…"

 SCP-1807-A refers to human voices that can be heard from within the anomalous interior of the okapi, hereby designated SCP-1807-1. As of the time of writing, twenty-seven (27) separate voices have been distinguished and subsequently matched to persons that have disappeared from the town of ██████████, Florida, including [REDACTED] Instances of SCP-1807-A can be heard communicating exclusively with each other, either due to inability to hear outside voices or unwillingness to acknowledge such things. See Audio Log-1807-Alpha for a sample of these voices.

I'm not sure the voices need their own SCP designation. They never occur outside the corpse, and they're not important enough or referenced frequently enough to require a permanent separate designation. Also, it gets confusing with all of the -As and -Bs and -1s. They could just be considered part of the ambiance to SCP-1807-1.

 SCP-1807-1 is the space within the okapi,

You already said that. I think you should move the above voice section down into the SCP-1807-1 section.

 It is cold within this space, as the highest recorded temperature is 0oC.

That's not clinical at all. Consider changing it to, "SCP-1807-1 maintains temperatures below 0oC by unknown means." or some such.

 There are several structures and machines of unknown purpose affixed to the various surfaces of SCP-1807-1. All of these objects are composed of a wide variety of organic matter. When first discovered, these machines displayed minimal levels of activity and complexity, and most appeared to be entirely nonfunctional; however, personnel began noticing increased activity from these objects as well as drastically greater intricacy in their component parts after the creation of several instances of SCP-1807-A. Tentative further testing confirmed a correlation between these two phenomena; as such, testing on SCP-1807 has been temporarily halted.

I think you need at least one extra sentence here. You don't really explain what the inside of this thing looks like at all. The Foundation must have SOME guess as to what they are. Do they look like organs? Have they been opened or examined or removed? Do any of them react to stimulus?

 On 16/02/2006, several humanoids attired in vintage diving costumes, hereby designated SCP-1807-B, manifested within Site 23 and demanded the usage of SCP-1807. The following log comprises the first interaction with these entities.

How did they manifest? Why can't they just manifest right next to SCP-1807?

Anyways, its such a great, creative idea, I really hope you manage to get this one perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is…strange. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a couple little things.

Instances of SCP-1807-A can be heard communicating exclusively with each other, either due to inability to hear outside voices or unwillingness to acknowledge such things.

"Such things" sounds a bit awkward to me. Maybe just them instead.

Also, might want to blackbox the date on the -B incident. The date says 06 but the quality of the photo seems to say otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll have to work on the language of the interview in the future, I'm afraid, but for now I can address at least the -A's and the backstory issues. For now, I've moved the location of the description of the -A's so that it's more clear; if it still seems iffy, I'll go back and edit it for clarity. As for the backstory, FFB above me roughly has it right. These guys have no clue what goes in a petting zoo, other than educated guesses; hell, they don't even really know what the animal is itself.

If I'm not exhausted/swamped tonight, I'll see what I can do to add to the story within the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, this article absolutely, positively, must be an okapi, but it might do well for everyone to have it just eat up the zoo veterinarian or something.

I disagree. I don't think it need necessarily be any animal at all over any other animal. The choice of vessel by these eldritch beings would ostensibly be random. And it actually makes some sense for them to dump an exotic animal in a petting zoo anyway, having absolutely no idea what animals would be appropriately found in such a setting in our world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so torn on this.

As a base idea, this is fantastic. This is a "10/10 would gallivant joyfully across the land with" idea.

Execution is what I am having difficulty with. With the ending interview, I was all excited for the big "OH I GET IT" moment, but that never came because you dumped all of it right there, without the flavorful grammar hiccups and whatnot.

Also, you handle the -A pretty awkwardly. They should be described when you first bring up the term, or at least in the paragraph following. This would probably ease up on the confusion as to what the thing is, as would details such as an ear tag from the petting zoo.

Which also outlines the problem of why there was an okami in a petting zoo to begin with. that is not a common thing to put in a petting zoo. I mean, this article absolutely, positively, must be an okapi, but it might do well for everyone to have it just eat up the zoo veterinarian or something.

I shall downvote for now, because this is not nearly as good as it could and should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad I found it. It actually inspired most of the background stuff about the -B's and helped me add a lot of depth to the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good, heheheh. Glad to see you used a picture I uploaded to the Visrecs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See my reply to ophite upthread for a rough explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that when I was reading through the draft, but I found that the last paragraph of the Description made it sufficiently clear with a little deliberate reading. Ymmv.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no clue what is going to on here. Sometimes that is a good thing, but this just seems way too disjointed and random to upvote. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it just me or is there no unambiguous description of what -A is?

Reading the commentary, if -As refer to the disappeared individuals, I may have a problem with that; it feels like those are rather just "affected individuals" rather than a core component of the SCP. Also, as with all multi-component SCPs, the okapi itself should be -1 or -A rather than 1807; 1807 should refer to the aggregate SCP including all its related sub-components.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. I really like the interviews.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally like specifying specific cells in my articles, just for precision's sake.

Well, that makes it so that it's clear that there's an extradimensional space in the okapi, not just a very big okapi.

I'll work on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well…

Maybe an authorial explanation can happen here?
Maybe?

So, the order of events:

  • -B's world goes to shit
  • They use some of their minimal "heat" to transport themselves to our world temporarily and deploy the okapi
    • Why the okapi? Because it doesn't matter if it was an okapi or an alpaca or a sheep, really. They just wanted something that people were likely to pet. As such…
  • They put it in a petting zoo.
  • Multiple disappearing children (and notable child molester) alarmed many people.
  • Eventually see that the okapi is causing this.
  • People kill the okapi because fucking hell it took their kids
  • Someone goes as far as trying to cut it open to try to get their kids back
  • Enter Foundation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Containment Cell #8467

Why that specific? Is that the official non-euclidian okapi corpse portal holding cell?

The object measures approximately 1m in height

For an okapi corpse, that seems rather…useless information.

remarkably cold

Not the best tone there.

I love it though. You write my kind of fucking weirdness. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hahaha.

Eheheh.

Heh.

What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Random for randomness' sake. Or like there's humor that's outside the narrative.

I like it. Don't get me wrong. But not quite enough to overcome some of the bothersome thematic details. (Again, IRA good, but not so good that it overcomes that problem.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's the sliiightest chance that I wrote that one, too :> Any what do you mean by that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my new favorite headcanon, this is in some way related to giraffe hell. Neutral vote, though, because while well done generally, some details felt like anomalous monkeycheese.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As mentioned in chat, I like it a lot even if it took me a read-through or two to get what was happening here. Oblique references to alternate realities/temporal travelers that kidnap people for fuel using Alpaca ships? Upvoted for the wonderful strange!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Credit goes to Technician Downs for the original idea. Thanks to everyone that helped me out with critiques in chat and forums for this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix'd. It's been so long since I've posted a skip ._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Item #: SCP-XXXX

You got 47 of those.
